title: PHONE_REGEX strictened — + prefix support removed
commit_message: fix: remove optional + prefix from PHONE_REGEX — accept local numbers only

why_fully_standalone.explanation: PHONE_REGEX is defined in constants.ts and used in phone-validation UI logic. In this test repository, no cross-dependent test references PHONE_REGEX in its symbols[] (phone validation is not cross-wired to a second file yet). No feature reducer uses PHONE_REGEX either. Therefore ALL critical tests are in the standalone category — exactly one describe block, one file, one symbol.
why_fully_standalone.pattern: changed_symbol  →  only standalone tests have it in symbols[]  →  zero cross-dependent, zero feature as CRITICAL

CRITICAL.reason: describe('PHONE_REGEX') label exactly matches changed symbol in standalone/regex.constants.test.js

tests.test: PHONE_REGEX > accepts a 10-digit phone number
PHONE_REGEX > accepts a phone number with a + country prefix
tests.result: ⚠️  WILL FAIL — + prefix removed from regex; '+919876543210' no longer matches

tests.test: PHONE_REGEX > rejects a phone number shorter than 10 digits

HIGH.reason: Same source file (src/types/constants.ts) changed — sibling regex constants may share test infrastructure or be accidentally broken by a typo in the diff

tests.test: EMAIL_REGEX > accepts a valid .com email address
tests.why: Sibling constant in same file — conservative inclusion

tests.test: EMAIL_REGEX > rejects an email address missing the @ symbol
EMAIL_REGEX > rejects a non-.com domain (e.g. .org)
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > accepts a password meeting all requirements
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without an uppercase letter
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without a special character
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password longer than 30 characters
CARD_REGEX > accepts a string of digits and spaces
CARD_REGEX > rejects letters inside a card number
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > accepts a valid alphanumeric username with a letter
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > rejects a username made entirely of digits
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > accepts a properly spaced 19-character card number
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes input containing @ to email validation and accepts a valid address
tests.why: sources include constants.ts — same file changed

tests.test: validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes plain text input to username validation and accepts a clean username
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the email format is invalid via EMAIL_REGEX
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the username contains special characters
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > output of generateCardNumber preserves first and last 4 digits
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > correctly masks middle digits with X characters
storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.stripePaymentDetails equals STRIPEPAYMENTDETAILS
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > accepts a standard 19-character card number with spaces
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number that contains letters

SKIP.reason: sources[] have zero overlap with src/types/constants.ts
skipped_files: test_repository/cross-dependent/auth-storage.cross.test.js  (isUserLoggedIn, updateNewTokenDetails, capitalizeFirstLetter describes — constants.ts not in sources[])
test_repository/cross-dependent/payment-state.cross.test.js (updateUserCards, updatePaymentPlanDetails, CLEARCARDS describes — constants.ts not in sources[])

coverage_gaps.test: PHONE_REGEX > accepts a phone number with a + country prefix
coverage_gaps.reason: Old regex: /^\+?[0-9]{10,15}$/ — allowed optional +.  New regex: /^[0-9]{10,15}$/ — + prefix now invalid. Test input '+919876543210' will not match.
coverage_gaps.action_required: Either: (a) update test to assert false for +prefix, OR (b) add a new test 'rejects a phone number with a + prefix'

selection_proof.note: All 3 critical tests come exclusively from the standalone category — zero cross-dependent, zero feature

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "PHONE_REGEX|EMAIL_REGEX|PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD|CARD_REGEX|UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX|validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX|generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX|storageKeys align with payment flow expectations|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input"
